1	'use strict'
2	
3	const fs = require('node:fs')
4	const path = require('node:path')
5	const { Stream, PassThrough, Writable } = require('node:stream')
6	
7	const ChunkEmitter = require('./lib/chunk-emitter')
8	const HeaderSkipper = require('./lib/header-skipper')
9	const LineTransformer = require('./lib/line-transformer')
10	
11	const STATE = { HEADERS: 1, BODY: 2 }
12	const MAX_IDX_KEYS = 1000
13	
14	class MessageStream extends Stream {
15	  // Public observable state
16	  uuid
17	  bytes_read = 0
18	  state = STATE.HEADERS
19	  idx = {}
20	  spooling = false
21	  buffered = 0
22	  total_buffered = 0
23	  max_data_inflight = 0
24	  headers
25	
26	  // Private write-side state
27	  #queue = []
28	  #writeCe = null
29	  #fd = null
30	  #ws = null
31	  #openPending = false
32	  #writePending = false
33	  #writeComplete = false
34	  #wsEnded = false
35	  #endCalled = false
36	  #endCallback = null
37	  #idxCount = 0
38	
39	  // Private read-side state
40	  #inPipe = false
41	  #currentSource = null
42	
43	  // Config
44	  #bufferMax
45	  #spoolDir
46	  #filename
47	
48	  constructor(cfg = {}, id, headers) {
49	    super()
50	    if (!id) throw new Error('id required')
51	    this.uuid = id
52	    this.headers = headers ?? []
53	    this.#bufferMax = !isNaN(cfg?.main?.spool_after)
54	      ? Number(cfg.main.spool_after)
55	      : -1
56	    this.#spoolDir = cfg?.main?.spool_dir ?? '/tmp'
57	    this.#filename = path.join(this.#spoolDir, `${path.basename(id)}.eml`)
58	  }
59	
60	  // ── Write side ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
61	
62	  add_line(line) {
63	    if (typeof line === 'string') line = Buffer.from(line)
64	
65	    if (!this.#writeCe) {
66	      this.#writeCe = new ChunkEmitter()
67	      this.#writeCe.on('data', (chunk) => this.#write(chunk))
68	    }
69	
70	    this.bytes_read += line.length
71	
72	    // Build up an index of 'interesting' data on the fly
73	    if (this.state === STATE.HEADERS) {
74	      // Look for end of headers line
75	      if (line.length === 2 && line[0] === 0x0d && line[1] === 0x0a) {
76	        this.idx.headers = { start: 0, end: this.bytes_read - line.length }
77	        this.#idxCount++
78	        this.state = STATE.BODY
79	        this.idx.body = { start: this.bytes_read }
80	        this.#idxCount++
81	      }
82	    }
83	
84	    if (this.state === STATE.BODY) {
85	      // Look for MIME boundaries
86	      if (line.length > 4 && line[0] === 0x2d && line[1] === 0x2d) {
87	        let boundary = line.slice(2).toString().trimEnd()
88	        if (/--\s*$/.test(line)) {
89	          boundary = boundary.slice(0, -2)
90	          if (this.idx[boundary]) this.idx[boundary].end = this.bytes_read
91	        } else {
92	          if (!this.idx[boundary] && this.#idxCount < MAX_IDX_KEYS) {
93	            this.idx[boundary] = { start: this.bytes_read - line.length }
94	            this.#idxCount++
95	          }
96	        }
97	      }
98	    }
99	
100	    this.#writeCe.fill(line)
101	  }
102	
103	  add_line_end(cb) {
104	    if (this.idx.body) this.idx.body.end = this.bytes_read
105	    this.#endCalled = true
106	    if (typeof cb === 'function') this.#endCallback = cb
107	    // Call _write() only if no new data was emitted This might happen if the
108	    // message size matches the size of the chunk buffer.
109	    if (!this.#writeCe.end()) this.#write()
110	  }
111	
112	  #write(data) {
113	    if (data) {

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `transaction.js`.

What changed:
- When `end_data()` handles a malformed/busted email with no explicit header/body separator, it now writes an inserted blank header/body separator plus the detected body lines into `message_stream`.
- This keeps `message_stream`’s raw stored message structure consistent with the parsed header state, so later `txn.add_header()` output is emitted in the header section instead of causing the body to be skipped/misplaced.

Validation performed:
- Added temporary regression tests reproducing the issue.
- Confirmed the malformed-message case failed before the fix.
- Confirmed it passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary tests as requested.
- Ran the transaction test suite:

```text
npx mocha test/transaction.js
28 passing
```

- Also ran broader transaction-related tests:

```text
npm test -- --grep transaction
32 passing
```

No commit was created.
